allies.rationale_text: Decision-trees strengthens multi-criteria-decision-analysis by making branches, outcomes, and trade-offs explicit before scoring.
allies.source_quote: MCDA can be visualized as a decision tree

allies.rationale_text: Opportunity-cost sharpens multi-criteria-decision-analysis by keeping trade-offs visible instead of pretending all criteria can be maximized together.
allies.source_quote: compare options transparently across competing goals

antagonists.rationale_text: Commitment-bias distorts multi-criteria-decision-analysis when the matrix is reverse-engineered to defend a preferred option.
antagonists.source_quote: criteria are subconsciously chosen or weighted to steer toward a desired conclusion

structured_tensions.tension_text: Multi-criteria-decision-analysis conflicts with commitment-bias when scoring weights are frozen around an already favored option.
structured_tensions.source_quote: multi-criteria-decision-analysis vs commitment-bias: scoring weights are frozen around an already favored option.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Multi-criteria-decision-analysis conflicts with representativeness-bias when salient criteria overshadow base-rate relevance.
structured_tensions.source_quote: multi-criteria-decision-analysis vs representativeness-bias: salient criteria overshadow base-rate relevance.

curation_notes.summary: Relation surface stays inside the current graph vocabulary and keeps omission stronger than weak forcing.
donor_drops: Did not widen Wave 3 target ids or import donor-shaped relation neighborhoods to rescue weak local grounding.
open_questions: Some source-real neighbors remain omitted because the current graph vocabulary would force weaker mappings than this conservative pass allows.

allies: Wave 4 candidacy remains observational only and is noted only in batch artifacts.
antagonists: No multihop or higher-order semantics were introduced in these Wave 3 files.
structured_tensions: Explicit tensions were preserved only where the raw source clearly earned a current-vocabulary target.
